text: Welcome to Year 3. First lab is the advanced wall framing section. Bring your tape square and a sharp pencil. Steel toes required in the shop.
Stair layout starts next week. Review rise and run before Tuesday. We will cut stringers in the shop so come ready to work.
Roof framing model build is due Feb 6. Get your rafter cuts checked by me before you assemble. No shortcuts on the birdsmouth.
Finish carpentry week. Coped joints beat caulk every time. Practice your cope cuts on scrap before the graded lab.
Base cabinet project posted. It is due in the fall term when we resume cabinet work. Start planning your cut list now if you want a head start.
OSHA 30 kicks off today. This card follows you your whole career so take it seriously. Modules are self paced but the deadlines are real.
Fall protection module due Nov 14. Falls are the number one killer in this trade. Know your anchor points.
Silica standard is on the PPE module. If you cut concrete or masonry dry you are asking for trouble. Wet cut and wear the mask.
The OSHA 30 final assessment opens in the fall for the renewal cohort. Finish your outstanding modules before then and you are all set for the card.
Welcome to Blueprint Reading. If you can read a set of plans you are worth more on any crew. We start with lines symbols and scale.
Floor plan takeoff due Jan 9. Get an architect scale if you do not own one yet. Do not eyeball dimensions.
Lumber takeoff is the last graded assignment of the term. This is the skill that gets you a lead role. Double check your board footage.
Floor and ceiling systems lab moved to next Thursday. Shop is tied up with the roof layouts. Bring your joist hangers and a framing hammer.
Door hanging lab this week. A prehung is easy, a slab in an old opening is the real test. We will do both. Bring shims.
Reminder on the roof framing quiz Friday. Know your common, hip, and jack rafters and how to read the rafter tables. No calculators, use the square.
Great work on the finish carpentry lab. Coped inside corners looked clean across the board. Photos of the graded joints are posted in the finish module.
Scaffolding and ladders unit is open. Read the competent person requirements. If you would not trust it with your kid on it, do not climb it.
Hazard communication module due Jan 23. Know how to read an SDS. Every product on your site has one for a reason.
Elevations and sections unit posted. An elevation shows you the face, a section shows you what is inside the wall. Learn to flip between them fast.
Details and dimensioning quiz Feb 6. If a dimension string does not add up to the overall, something is wrong. Always check the math on the plans.
